title: Define Toggle Symbols
text: Define Toggle Symbols Toggle symbols are language setups that define the default text for askmess answers, and the labels of the Character Attributes menu. They are not applicable to web or mobile applications. To define your own toggle symbols: Choose More Editors > Toggle Symbols . Select or create a message library to hold the language setup. Select the language for which you are defining toggle symbols. To define the texts for the Character Attributes menu, enter the text you want to appear on the menu when that item is toggled on and off Toggle On and Toggle Off fields. For example, in the USA language setup, the BOLD item has the text no Bold when it is toggled off and Bold when it is toggled on. For the FONT action, the text you enter in the Toggle On field is used, for example, Courier or Helvetica. Specify the default replies for an askmess in the Default askmess Answers list box. You can define up to two sets of Positive (toggle on) and Negative (toggle off) replies that are recognized by the ProcScript askmess statement. For example, if you are defining default askmess answers for a character mode application, you can capitalize the characters a user must enter. For character mode applications, you can also allow alternative values to be entered as affirmative and negative replies to an askmess . Related Links askmess Display a message and wait for the user's response. Was this topic helpful? Like Dislike
